story.iWant: tickets to be automatically assigned to the appropriate team or agent based on configurable routing rules
story.soThat: incidents and service requests reach the right resolver quickly without manual triage, reducing First Response Time and improving SLA compliance

tasks.task: Database Schema and Seed Data (AC: 9, 10) - 2 hours - Create Flyway migration V13__create_teams_table.sql - Create Flyway migration V14__add_team_id_to_routing_rules.sql - Insert seed data for default teams (Network, Software, Hardware, General Support) - Test migrations with ./gradlew flywayMigrate

Team Domain Entity (AC: 8, 14) - 2 hours - Create Team.java entity in internal/domain/ package - Add JPA annotations, UUIDv7 generator, lifecycle methods - Create TeamTest with 3 unit tests - Add Team to RoutingRule entity as @ManyToOne relationship

Team Repository (AC: 7) - 1 hour - Create TeamRepository interface in internal/repository/ package - Define query method: findByEnabledTrue() - Repository verified through integration tests (Testcontainers)

TicketCardRepository Round-Robin Query (AC: 11) - 2 hours - Add findAgentWithFewestTickets(UUID teamId) method to TicketCardRepository - Implement custom @Query with GROUP BY and ORDER BY COUNT - Unit test with mock data (3 agents with different ticket counts) - Integration test with Testcontainers verifying correct agent selected

RoutingEngine Service Implementation (AC: 1-5) - 6 hours - Create RoutingEngine service in internal/service/ package - Implement applyRules(Ticket ticket) method - Implement matches(RoutingRule rule, Ticket ticket) with switch for condition types - Implement assignByRule(RoutingRule rule, Ticket ticket) with team vs agent logic - Implement selectAgentRoundRobin(Team team) calling TicketCardRepository - Add Javadoc with examples and edge case notes - Create RoutingEngineTest with 6 unit tests

TicketService Integration (AC: 6) - 3 hours - Inject RoutingEngine into TicketService - Modify createTicket() to call routingEngine.applyRules(ticket) after SLA creation - Add try-catch for routing exceptions (log warning, continue) - Verify TicketCreatedEvent includes assigneeId if auto-assigned - Update TicketServiceTest to verify routing integration

Integration Tests (AC: 12) - 6 hours - Create RoutingEngineIntegrationTest with Testcontainers PostgreSQL - Implement IT-ROUTING-1 to IT-ROUTING-8 (8 test scenarios) - Seed test data: teams, agents, routing rules - Verify ticket assignment after creation - Test edge cases: no matching rule, disabled rules, round-robin

Documentation and Review (AC: all) - 2 hours - Create ADR: "ADR-012: Routing Engine and Auto-Assignment Strategy" - Update docs/architecture/data-model.md with teams table schema - Update Epic 2 Tech Spec completion status (Story 2.4 → In Progress) - Code review: verify all ACs met, test coverage >80%

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Create RoutingEngine service at backend/src/main/java/io/monosense/synergyflow/itsm/internal/service/RoutingEngine.java: - @Service annotation with package-private visibility - Method: void applyRules(Ticket ticket) - Evaluates routing rules and auto-assigns ticket - Method: boolean matches(RoutingRule rule, Ticket ticket) - Checks if rule matches ticket criteria - Method: void assignByRule(RoutingRule rule, Ticket ticket) - Assigns ticket to target team or agent - Method: UUID selectAgentRoundRobin(Team team) - Selects agent with fewest assigned tickets in team - Inject dependencies: RoutingRuleRepository, TicketService, TeamRepository, TicketCardRepository

Implement condition type matching: - CATEGORY: Matches if rule.getConditionValue().equals(ticket.getCategory()) - PRIORITY: Matches if rule.getConditionValue().equals(ticket.getPriority().name()) - SUBCATEGORY: Matches if rule.getConditionValue().equals(ticket.getSubcategory()) - ROUND_ROBIN: Always matches (used as fallback rule)

Rules evaluated in priority order: - Fetch enabled routing rules ordered by priority ASC: RoutingRuleRepository.findEnabledRulesByPriority() - Iterate through rules in order - First matching rule wins - assign ticket and exit - If no rules match, leave ticket unassigned

Support team-based assignment: - If rule.getTargetTeamId() != null, select agent within team using round-robin - Round-robin strategy: TicketCardRepository.findAgentWithFewestTickets(teamId) returns agent with lowest workload - Call TicketService.assignTicket(ticketId, agentId, "Auto-assigned by routing rule: " + rule.getRuleName())

Support direct agent assignment: - If rule.getTargetAgentId() != null, assign directly to specific agent - Call TicketService.assignTicket(ticketId, rule.getTargetAgentId(), "Auto-assigned by routing rule: " + rule.getRuleName()) - Skip round-robin for direct agent rules

Integrate with TicketService.createTicket(): - After ticket persistence and SLA creation, call routingEngine.applyRules(ticket) - Auto-assignment happens before TicketCreatedEvent is published (event includes assigneeId if auto-assigned) - If routing engine throws exception, log warning and continue (ticket remains unassigned, event still published)

Create TeamRepository at backend/src/main/java/io/monosense/synergyflow/itsm/internal/repository/TeamRepository.java: - Extends JpaRepository<Team, UUID> - Package-private visibility (internal repository) - Query method: List<Team> findByEnabledTrue() (for future UI)

Create Team entity at backend/src/main/java/io/monosense/synergyflow/itsm/internal/domain/Team.java: - UUIDv7 primary key with @UuidGenerator(style = Style.VERSION_7) - Fields: id (UUID), name (String, not null, unique), description (String), enabled (boolean, default true), createdAt (Instant), updatedAt (Instant) - @Table(name = "teams") with package-private visibility - @PrePersist and @PreUpdate lifecycle methods for timestamps

Create Flyway migration V13__create_teams_table.sql: - Table teams with columns: id (UUID PK), name (VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE), description (TEXT), enabled (BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE), created_at (TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL), updated_at (TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE NOT NULL) - Index on name for uniqueness check: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_teams_name ON teams(name); - Seed data: Insert default teams (Network Team, Software Team, Hardware Team, General Support)

Add teamId field to RoutingRule entity: - Create Flyway migration V14__add_team_id_to_routing_rules.sql - Add column: team_id (UUID, nullable, FK to teams table) - Foreign key: FOREIGN KEY (team_id) REFERENCES teams(id) ON DELETE SET NULL - Nullable because rules can target specific agent instead of team

Add TicketCardRepository.findAgentWithFewestTickets(UUID teamId) method: - Custom query: @Query("SELECT t.assigneeId FROM TicketCard t WHERE t.teamId = :teamId AND t.status NOT IN ('RESOLVED', 'CLOSED') GROUP BY t.assigneeId ORDER BY COUNT(t.id) ASC LIMIT 1") - Returns UUID of agent with fewest open tickets in team - Used for round-robin load balancing

Create RoutingEngineIntegrationTest.java: - IT-ROUTING-1: Auto-assign by category - Create incident with category="Network Issue" → verify auto-assigned to Network Team agent - IT-ROUTING-2: Auto-assign by priority - Create CRITICAL incident → verify auto-assigned to Senior Agent - IT-ROUTING-3: First rule wins - Create ticket matching multiple rules → verify assigned by first matching rule (lowest priority number) - IT-ROUTING-4: No matching rule - Create ticket with no matching rules → verify ticket remains unassigned - IT-ROUTING-5: Round-robin load balancing - Create 3 tickets matching same team rule → verify distributed across 3 agents - IT-ROUTING-6: Direct agent assignment - Create ticket with rule targeting specific agent → verify assigned to that agent (no round-robin) - IT-ROUTING-7: Disabled rule skipped - Create ticket matching disabled rule → verify rule skipped, next rule applied - IT-ROUTING-8: Round-robin fallback - Create ticket with ROUND_ROBIN rule → verify assigned to agent with fewest tickets

Create RoutingEngineTest.java: - UT-ROUTING-1: CATEGORY match - Verify matches(categoryRule, ticket) returns true if categories match - UT-ROUTING-2: PRIORITY match - Verify matches(priorityRule, ticket) returns true if priorities match - UT-ROUTING-3: SUBCATEGORY match - Verify matches(subcategoryRule, ticket) returns true if subcategories match - UT-ROUTING-4: ROUND_ROBIN always matches - Verify matches(roundRobinRule, ticket) always returns true - UT-ROUTING-5: No match - Verify matches(categoryRule, ticketWithDifferentCategory) returns false - UT-ROUTING-6: Round-robin selection - Mock TicketCardRepository, verify selectAgentRoundRobin() calls findAgentWithFewestTickets()

Create TeamTest.java: - UT-TEAM-1: Entity creation - Verify Team entity created with UUIDv7, timestamps set - UT-TEAM-2: PrePersist lifecycle - Verify createdAt and updatedAt set on persist - UT-TEAM-3: PreUpdate lifecycle - Verify updatedAt updated on entity modification

doc.title: Epic 2 ITSM Tech Spec
doc.snippet: Story 2.4 defines routing engine implementation with auto-assignment based on category, priority, round-robin within teams

doc.title: Epic 2 ITSM Tech Spec
doc.snippet: RoutingEngine.java service implementation with applyRules(), matches(), assignByRule(), selectAgentRoundRobin() methods. Evaluates rules in priority order, supports CATEGORY/PRIORITY/SUBCATEGORY/ROUND_ROBIN condition types

doc.title: Epic 2 ITSM Tech Spec
doc.snippet: TicketService.createTicket() calls routingEngine.applyRules(ticket) after SLA creation and before TicketCreatedEvent publication

doc.title: ADR 009: Integration Test Requirements (Testcontainers)
doc.snippet: All integration tests MUST use Testcontainers for PostgreSQL, Redis. @DynamicPropertySource configuration with container endpoints. Container reuse enabled locally via ~/.testcontainers.properties

doc.title: Module Boundaries
doc.snippet: ITSM module dependencies: security, eventing, sla, sse, audit. Cross-module calls via @NamedInterface SPI packages. internal/ packages not accessible from other modules

doc.title: Story 2.2 REVISED (Approved)
doc.snippet: TicketService is the entry point for ticket creation, implements business logic for validation, SLA creation, event publication. Package-private visibility in internal.service package

doc.title: Story 2.3 (Completed)
doc.snippet: SLA tracking with priority-based deadlines, TimerService for scheduling, SlaTracker entity with response/resolution deadlines. Integration with TicketService for auto-creation on ticket creation

artifact.reason: Existing routing rule entity with UUIDv7, condition types (CATEGORY/PRIORITY/SUBCATEGORY/ROUND_ROBIN), target team/agent fields. Story 2.4 adds teamId FK via migration V14
Fetches enabled routing rules in priority order (ASC). Used by RoutingEngine.applyRules() to evaluate rules in precedence order
Enum defining routing condition types: CATEGORY, PRIORITY, SUBCATEGORY, ROUND_ROBIN. Used in RoutingEngine.matches() switch logic
Entry point for ticket creation. Story 2.4 AC-6: inject RoutingEngine, call routingEngine.applyRules(ticket) after SLA creation (line 178) and before event publication (line 193)
Ticket assignment method. Called by RoutingEngine.assignByRule() for auto-assignment with reason "Auto-assigned by routing rule: <ruleName>"
Read model repository for ticket cards. Story 2.4 AC-11: add findAgentWithFewestTickets(UUID teamId) custom query for round-robin load balancing
Base ticket entity with fields: id, ticketType, title, description, status, priority, category, subcategory, requesterId, assigneeId. Used for routing condition matching

dependency.note: Required for UUIDv7 generator (@UuidGenerator with Style.VERSION_7)

constraints.constraint: Package-private visibility: All new classes (RoutingEngine, Team, TeamRepository) must have package-private visibility (no public modifier) in internal.* packages
UUIDv7 primary keys: All entities must use @UuidGenerator(style = Style.VERSION_7) for time-ordered identifiers
Lifecycle timestamps: All entities must implement @PrePersist and @PreUpdate callbacks to manage createdAt/updatedAt timestamps
Testcontainers mandatory: All integration tests MUST use Testcontainers for PostgreSQL (no H2/in-memory databases) per ADR-009
Module boundaries: RoutingEngine is internal ITSM implementation, NOT exposed via SPI. Other modules cannot access routing logic directly
First rule wins: Routing rules evaluated in priority order (ASC), first matching rule assigns ticket and exits evaluation loop
Round-robin fairness: selectAgentRoundRobin() must use TicketCardRepository.findAgentWithFewestTickets() to distribute load evenly across team agents
Exception handling: If RoutingEngine.applyRules() throws exception, log warning and continue (ticket remains unassigned, event still published) per AC-6
Event ordering: Auto-assignment must occur AFTER SLA creation and BEFORE TicketCreatedEvent publication (event includes assigneeId if auto-assigned)
Testing standards: >80% code coverage required for RoutingEngine and Team entities. All AC test cases (UT-ROUTING-1 to UT-ROUTING-6, IT-ROUTING-1 to IT-ROUTING-8) must pass

interface.name: RoutingRuleRepository.findByEnabledTrueOrderByPriorityAsc()
interface.usage: Called by RoutingEngine.applyRules() to fetch enabled routing rules in priority order for evaluation

interface.name: TicketService.assignTicket()
interface.signature: Ticket assignTicket(UUID ticketId, UUID assigneeId, UUID currentUserId)
interface.usage: Called by RoutingEngine.assignByRule() to auto-assign ticket. Pass currentUserId as system/routing engine identifier, assigneeId from round-robin or direct rule

interface.name: TicketCardRepository.findAgentWithFewestTickets()
interface.usage: Custom query for round-robin load balancing. Returns agent UUID with fewest open tickets in team. Query: SELECT t.assigneeId FROM TicketCard t WHERE t.teamId = :teamId AND t.status NOT IN ('RESOLVED', 'CLOSED') GROUP BY t.assigneeId ORDER BY COUNT(t.id) ASC LIMIT 1

interface.name: SlaCalculator.calculateDueAt()
interface.usage: Already used by TicketService.createTicket() for SLA tracking. Not modified in Story 2.4

interface.name: EventPublisher.publish()
interface.signature: void publish(UUID aggregateId, String aggregateType, String eventType, Long version, Instant occurredAt, ObjectNode payload)
interface.usage: Already used by TicketService for TicketCreatedEvent. Not modified in Story 2.4

tests.standards: Integration tests MUST use Testcontainers (PostgreSQL 16) per ADR-009. Configure containers via @DynamicPropertySource. Container reuse enabled locally (~/.testcontainers.properties: testcontainers.reuse.enable=true). Unit tests use Mockito for repository/service mocking. Test naming: UT-* (unit), IT-* (integration). Coverage target: >80% for RoutingEngine, Team entities. All tests extend base test classes for consistent setup. Use @SpringBootTest for integration tests, @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) for unit tests.

locations.location: backend/src/test/resources/db/testdata/ (test SQL seed data for teams, agents, routing rules)

ideas.test: Unit test: CATEGORY condition matching - create categoryRule with conditionValue="Network", ticket with category="Network", assert matches() returns true
Unit test: PRIORITY condition matching - create priorityRule with conditionValue="CRITICAL", ticket with priority=CRITICAL, assert matches() returns true
Unit test: SUBCATEGORY condition matching - create subcategoryRule with conditionValue="VPN", ticket with subcategory="VPN", assert matches() returns true
Unit test: ROUND_ROBIN always matches - create roundRobinRule, any ticket, assert matches() returns true
Unit test: No match - create categoryRule with conditionValue="Network", ticket with category="Software", assert matches() returns false
Unit test: Round-robin selection - mock TicketCardRepository.findAgentWithFewestTickets(teamId) to return agentId, verify selectAgentRoundRobin() calls repository and returns correct agent

Integration test: Auto-assign by category - seed Network Team + 1 agent, seed routing rule (category="Network Issue" → Network Team), create incident with category="Network Issue", verify ticket.assigneeId == Network Team agent
Integration test: Auto-assign by priority - seed Senior Agent, seed routing rule (priority="CRITICAL" → Senior Agent), create CRITICAL incident, verify ticket.assigneeId == Senior Agent

Integration test: First rule wins - seed 2 rules (rule1: category="Network" priority=1, rule2: priority="CRITICAL" priority=2), create ticket matching both, verify assigned by rule1 (lower priority number)
Integration test: No matching rule - seed rule for category="Network", create ticket with category="Software", verify ticket.assigneeId == null (unassigned)
Integration test: Round-robin load balancing - seed General Support Team + 3 agents, seed ROUND_ROBIN rule → General Support, create 3 tickets, verify distributed across 3 agents (each has 1 ticket)
Integration test: Direct agent assignment - seed routing rule (targetAgentId=specificAgentId, no teamId), create matching ticket, verify ticket.assigneeId == specificAgentId (no round-robin)
Integration test: Disabled rule skipped - seed 2 rules (rule1: enabled=false priority=1, rule2: enabled=true priority=2), create ticket matching both, verify assigned by rule2 (rule1 skipped)
Integration test: Round-robin fallback - seed ROUND_ROBIN rule (priority=999), create ticket with no category/priority match, verify assigned to agent with fewest tickets via fallback rule
Unit test: Team entity creation - create Team("Network Team", "desc", true), verify id is UUIDv7, createdAt/updatedAt set
Unit test: Team @PrePersist lifecycle - create Team, call onCreate(), verify createdAt == updatedAt == now()
Unit test: Team @PreUpdate lifecycle - create Team, update name, call onUpdate(), verify updatedAt > createdAt
